name: MiniMax-M2
archDisplay: 62-layer decoder-only Transformer, 256 experts (top-8, sigmoid gating) + Multi-Token Prediction module, full GQA attention
note: Compact 229.9B-total/9.8B-active MoE tuned for coding and agentic workflows with interleaved <think> reasoning; original M2 release is MIT-licensed (later M2.x point releases, e.g. M2.1/M2.7, moved to a restrictive 'Modified-MIT').
tpsNote: Unsloth's UD-IQ4_XS dynamic GGUF (~108GB) is reported at ~15-25 tok/s on a single 16GB GPU + 96GB system RAM or Mac unified memory - no RTX-4090-specific figure found.

results.notes: GPQA-Diamond; identical figure independently confirmed on the HF model card's 'Intelligence Benchmarks' table.
BrowseComp (English); confirmed identically on the HF model card's 'Coding & Agentic Benchmarks' table. A separate BrowseComp-zh score of 48.5 also exists but has no distinct taxonomy slug.
Confirmed identically on the HF model card.

τ²-Bench-Telecom, confirmed identically on the HF model card. Distinct from the generic τ²-Bench (77.2) also reported, which mixes airline/retail/telecom domains and doesn't cleanly match either the tau-bench or tau2-bench-telecom taxonomy definitions, so that figure was left out.

verificationNotes: Confirmed real; official minimax.io/news/minimax-m2 post is literally dated 2025.10.27, matching releaseDate exactly (MarkTechPost/Simon Willison coverage followed a day or two later). 229.9B total/9.8B active MIT-licensed MoE confirmed via GitHub repo and MarkTechPost. All 7 benchmark rows (SWE-bench 69.4, LiveCodeBench 83, MMLU-Pro 82, AIME25 78, Terminal-Bench 46.3, GAIA 75.7, HLE 31.8) confirmed literally on the GitHub README. Independently confirmed the note's claim that later M2.x point releases (M2.1, M2.7) carry a distinct 'Modified-MIT' license requiring UI attribution, unlike the base M2's plain MIT.
